The jaw thrust maneuver (option A) involves moving the mandible forward without extending or rotating it. This action can help clear airway obstruction by pushing the base of the tongue off the posterior pharyngeal wall, thus opening up the upper airway space. The triple maneuver combines chin lift with head tilt and jaw thrust which together effectively mobilize structures like the tongue that may be obstructing the airway. raw_response:
The jaw thrust maneuver (option A) involves moving the mandible forward without extending or rotating it. This action can help clear airway obstruction by pushing the base of the tongue off the posterior pharyngeal wall, thus opening up the upper airway space. The triple maneuver combines chin lift with head tilt and jaw thrust which together effectively mobilize structures like the tongue that may be obstructing the airway. Given these descriptions, I would choose option B as this combination specifically targets movement of the tongue relative to the pharyngeal walls for clearing an airway obstruction.
Answer Choice: B To further clarify why 'Triple Maneuver' (Option B) is selected over other choices:

- It's designed explicitly to address obstructions including those caused by displacement of soft tissues such as the tongue against the back wall of the throat - making it ideal for scenarios requiring precise manipulation of oral/pharyngeal spaces. Answer Choice: B Triple Maneuver
